Luminance

Typographic space Playing with letters and shapes

Published Written
Updated Changed

A personal exploration of how type occupies physical space. Generative compositions treating letterforms as architecture. A fun art project where I turned letters into buildings and shapes. The computer helped me make patterns out of words.

This started as a weekend experiment and became a recurring creative outlet. The premise is simple: what if you treated a letterform not as a symbol but as a floor plan? Each glyph becomes a room, a corridor, a structural element. The compositions are generated programmatically but curated by hand.[1]

There's no practical application here. That's the point. Not everything needs to ship. Sometimes the most useful thing you can do as a designer is make something that exists only because you were curious.[2]

Changelog What changed

2024.11.10

Initial exploration.

References Where I learned this

  • [1]

    Inspired by Zuzana Licko's work on the relationship between type design and architectural thinking.

    Return to text Go back
  • [2]

    Frank Chimero's The Shape of Design makes this case more eloquently than I can.
